symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly progressive deep angioedema of the lips and face

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep facial/labial angioedema is the characteristic distribution of bradykinin-mediated angioedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-pruritic, tense, burning swelling with no urticaria or wheals

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of urticaria and pruritus points to a bradykinin rather than a histamine mechanism.
Histaminergic reactions typically produce itch and hives, which are absent here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent commencement of an ACE inhibitor (perindopril) ~10 days prior

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A temporally-related ACE inhibitor is the causative trigger; identifying it is pivotal to management.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of systemic anaphylaxis features (no generalised urticaria, wheeze, faintness, GI symptoms)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of multi-system involvement excludes anaphylaxis by clinical criteria.

symptoms.symptom_name: Current airway patency by self-report (speaking full sentences, no stridor or breathlessness)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A currently clear airway does not change the diagnosis and does not exclude developing internal (laryngeal) oedema; it is reassuring but not protective.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oropharyngeal examination normal (uvula, soft palate, posterior pharynx without oedema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear oropharynx lowers immediate airway risk — but this is a clinician examination sealed from the AI Doctor, who cannot obtain it via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear breath sounds bilaterally with no inspiratory stridor or wheeze on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of wheeze/stridor argues against lower- and upper-airway obstruction — a sealed auscultation finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 145/88, HR 104, RR 18, SpO2 98%, afebrile (36.9 C)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normotension, normal SpO2 and absence of fever argue against distributive shock, sepsis or cellulitis — clinician-measured values sealed from the AI Doctor; mild tachycardia is consistent with distress/anxiety.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum mast-cell tryptase (acute, ideally 1-3h from onset)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Normal tryptase supports a bradykinin (ACEi) mechanism and helps exclude a mast-cell/anaphylactic process; typically drawn in hospital and not available in real time via telehealth.
Screens for hereditary or acquired C1-INH deficiency if angioedema recurs after ACE inhibitor cessation.
Direct airway visualisation is indicated in hospital if any airway symptoms develop; it is a specialist procedure, never available via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine allergen skin-prick / specific IgE testing in the acute setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The mechanism is non-allergic (bradykinin-mediated); acute allergy testing will not change management and risks anchoring on an allergic cause.
